text: This video presents Uniportal Pure Robotic Assisted Thoracic Surgery for Left Bisegmentectomy S9-S10.
The incision is made in the 7th intercostal space between anterior and lateral axillary lines.
The active robotic arms are 1, 2, and 3, with number 4 being excluded.
The bissegmentectomy S9-S10 was chosen due to the post-COVID bilateral secular lesions and reduced pulmonary function.
Intrathoracic inspection reveals the slightly retracted visceral pleura corresponding to the level of the pulmonary lesion.
A complete inferior approach was preferred for this case after careful analysis of the arterial and venous branching on the preoperatory CT scan.
Dissection is started from the pulmonary ligament and surrounds the inferior part of the hilum, with consequent exposure and dissection of the inferior left pulmonary vein.
Station 9 lymph nodes are dissected and removed.
Dissection with fine energy grasping and dissecting instruments is advanced and the inferior basal vein is exposed, encircled with a short tape and stapled.
Just behind the vein stump, the B10 bronchus is evidenced, dissected, encircled and stapled.
The same approach is used for the B9 bronchus.
The individual bronchial anatomy was identified from the preoperatory bronchoscopy, which is advisable to be performed or repeated on the operating table just before starting the surgery.
Continuing the inferior approach, the arterial branches for the segments 10 and 9 are distally dissected in section, holding the distal bronchial stumps.
Here, a vascular staple is used for the A10.
Another two arterial branches for segment 9 were dissected and we used two ligating clips proximally and the ultrasonic vessel sealer distally.
For the parenchyma stapling, we used again the information from the careful analysis of the preoperatory CT scan images and the intraoperatory delimitation of anatomical marks on the visceral pleura.
In the anterior part, segment 9 was separated from segment 8.
In the posterior part, segment 10 was separated from segment 6, finishing the bisegmentectomy S9-S10 and removing the segments from the wound.
The integrity of the bronchial stumps were checked using C-line solution and inflating the lung.
Post-operatory chest X-ray shows complete pulmonary expansion.
There were no air leaks.
The chest tube was removed on the first post-operative day and the patient was discharged on the second post-operative day with excellent recovery.
Thank you.
